headline: Soaring Power Prices Have US Politicians Searching for Solutions
summary: The Energy Department has loaned $26.5 billion — the most in its history — to help a big Southeastern utility reduce the cost of building new power plants in Georgia and Alabama.  Meanwhile, the nation’s biggest grid operator has proposed capping wholesale prices and some governors are weighing utility rate freezes as a way to keep a lid on electricity costs.  The affordability problem is prompting a White House meeting Wednesday with technology executives.

content.title: Soaring Power Prices Have US Politicians Searching for Solutions

Meanwhile, the nation’s biggest grid operator has proposed capping wholesale prices and some governors are weighing utility rate freezes as a way to keep a lid on electricity costs.  The affordability problem is prompting a White House meeting Wednesday with technology executives.

headline: Binarly Announces Leadership Transition as Enterprise Demand Accelerates for Supply-Chain Security
summary: SANTA MONICA, Calif., March 04, 2026--Binarly, the industry leader in software and firmware supply‑chain security, today announced a leadership transition as the company enters its next phase of growth. Founder and current CEO Alex Matrosov has joined the company’s Board, and Gwenyth Castro has joined as Chief Executive Officer to scale global go-to-market and enterprise growth.

content.title: Binarly Announces Leadership Transition as Enterprise Demand Accelerates for Supply-Chain Security
content.summary: SANTA MONICA, Calif., March 04, 2026--Binarly, the industry leader in software and firmware supply‑chain security, today announced a leadership transition as the company enters its next phase of growth. Founder and current CEO Alex Matrosov has joined the company’s Board, and Gwenyth Castro has joined as Chief Executive Officer to scale global go-to-market and enterprise growth.

headline: Soaring Power Prices Send US Politicians on Search for Solutions
summary: The Energy Department has loaned $26.5 billion — the most in its history — to help a big Southeastern utility reduce the cost of building new power plants in Georgia and Alabama.  Meanwhile, the nation’s biggest grid operator has proposed capping wholesale prices and some governors are weighing utility rate freezes as a way to keep a lid on electricity costs.  The affordability problem has prompted a White House meeting Wednesday with technology executives to address price spikes tied to billions of dollars in artificial intelligence investments.

content.title: Soaring Power Prices Send US Politicians on Search for Solutions
content.summary: The Energy Department has loaned $26.5 billion — the most in its history — to help a big Southeastern utility reduce the cost of building new power plants in Georgia and Alabama.  Meanwhile, the nation’s biggest grid operator has proposed capping wholesale prices and some governors are weighing utility rate freezes as a way to keep a lid on electricity costs.  The affordability problem has prompted a White House meeting Wednesday with technology executives to address price spikes tied to billions of dollars in artificial intelligence investments.
